regime_justification: USDJPY near 160 watch-line, MOF Katayama warning of one-sided moves, BOJ meeting April 27-28 next week holds policy decision risk, oil elevated near $97 WTI prolonging inflation pressure, but vol subdued and no imminent trigger

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY at 159.82 approaches 160 MOF intervention threshold, Finance Minister Katayama publicly warned against one-sided yen weakness and expressed deep concern in Washington, BOJ meeting in 3 days with policy hold expected but June hike possible, risk reversals unavailable but carry remains under structural stress

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ meeting April 27-28 catalyst risk, USDJPY at watch-line, MOF vocal but no intervention yet, oil $97 keeping inflation elevated

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Post-BOJ positioning plus any Ueda signal on June normalization; USDJPY stretched but spread still 194 bps supporting carry

3w.label: Unlikely
3w.driver: BOJ event risk behind, Fed holds steady at 3.50-3.75%, oil stabilizes under ceasefire extension; carry resets lower

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: June BOJ meeting too far to price, Fed on hold, oil risks fading; fragile but contained absent new shock

bottom_line: Stage 2 Pre-Intervention: USDJPY 159.82 at MOF red-line, Katayama vocal, BOJ meets Mon-Tue next week. 1-2w grade C (Watchful) on event risk and stretched FX, but vol low (implied ~7.7%, VIX 19.3, MOVE 69.9) and spreads still 194 bps. Applied unwind prior to hold C through 2w; absent prior would grade D near-term. Oil $97 (¥/bbl 15,489) and Fed pause sustain pressure but no acute trigger. Weeks 3-4 grade D as BOJ event clears and setup diffuses. Watch MOF rate-checks, Ueda June signal, and USDJPY 160 breach for stage-3 entry.

narrative.p1: USDJPY climbed to 159.82 (+0.14% today, +0.38% over 5d), pressing the 160 MOF intervention watch-line. Finance Minister Katayama told reporters in Washington last week she conveyed 'deep concern' over yen depreciation and warned against one-sided moves, echoing classic pre-intervention rhetoric. Oil rallied to $97 WTI (¥/bbl now 15,489) as Trump extended the Iran ceasefire indefinitely but the Strait of Hormuz blockade persists. JGB 10Y yield rose 6 bps to 2.44% on the back of core CPI accelerating to 1.8% (first uptick in 5 months), while US 10Y climbed 8 bps to 4.31% on inflation concerns. The US-JP spread compressed 2 bps to 187 bps but remains supportive of carry. BOJ meets Monday-Tuesday next week (April 27-28); consensus is hold at 0.75%, but Ueda's April 13 speech flagged Middle East uncertainty and kept June normalization on the table. Stage upgraded from 1 (Pressure Building) to 2 (Pre-Intervention) on MOF vocal warnings and USDJPY proximity to 160.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP spread 187 bps, carry-to-vol ~24.3 (187/7.7), ¥/bbl 15,489 (+320 today), USDJPY 5d pace +0.38% (below MOF panic threshold of ~1%/week). Regime sits at Stage 2 Pre-Intervention: USDJPY at watch-line, MOF rhetoric escalating, BOJ event risk in 3 days, but vol subdued (USDJPY 1M implied ~7.7%, VIX 19.3, MOVE 69.9). Applied unwind prior to grade 1w and 2w as C (Watchful); absent the prior, near-term data alone would argue D (contained vol, spread still wide, no acute trigger). The prior acts as tiebreaker given proximity to known catalysts (BOJ meeting, 160 level, MOF warnings). Weeks 3-4 downgraded to D as BOJ event clears and oil likely stabilizes under extended ceasefire. Spread compression and oil elevation are the slow burns; intervention or BOJ surprise are the fast catalysts. The setup is fragile but not yet firing.
narrative.p3: Crypto data unavailable (BTC/XRP spot, OI, BTC-DXY correlation not retrieved in search). Absent fresh signals, infer consolidation: elevated oil and rates typically weigh on risk assets, but VIX sub-20 and equity resilience suggest liquidity remains ample. If BTC held range near recent levels, it implies crypto traders are not front-running a macro unwind—yet. A BOJ hold Monday + USDJPY retreat from 160 would be risk-on supportive; a surprise hike or MOF intervention would trigger deleveraging and BTC likely drains alongside equities. For positioning: trim leverage into the BOJ meeting, watch for any spike in BTC funding rates or OI collapse as early warning of liquidation cascade. The yen carry unwind thesis remains the dominant medium-term risk, but this week's catalyst is binary (BOJ) not structural (spread collapse).

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Both weeks grade C (Watchful) because BOJ meeting April 27-28 sits inside 1w window and post-meeting positioning bleeds into 2w. USDJPY at 160 watch-line and MOF vocal warnings elevate intervention risk. Oil $97 sustains inflation pressure. Vol is subdued but event risk is binary. Applied unwind prior as tiebreaker; data alone would argue D.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Grade steps down from C to D as BOJ event passes and uncertainty resolves. If BOJ holds and signals June hike (consensus), market reprices carry higher; if surprise hike, unwind occurs in week 2 and stabilizes by week 3. Oil likely consolidates under ceasefire extension. Spread at 187 bps still supports carry absent shock.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Both weeks grade D (Unlikely) as near-term catalysts clear. Fed holds April 29, BOJ decision behind, oil stabilizes. USDJPY either retreats from 160 (intervention/jawbone) or consolidates above if BOJ dovish. June BOJ meeting (June 15-16) too far to price. Setup remains fragile (tight spread, elevated oil, fiscal concerns) but contained absent new trigger.

watch_list: MOF rate-check rumors or Katayama escalation (intervention trigger)
BOJ meeting April 27-28 decision and Ueda press conference tone on June path
Iran ceasefire breakdown or Hormuz escalation driving oil above $100
USDJPY 1M risk reversals if data becomes available (JPY-call skew = hedging surge)
JGB 10Y yield break above 2.50% (fiscal stress signal)
BTC open interest collapse or funding rate spike (crypto leverage unwind early warning)
